question: What is an alpha,alpha-disubstituted amino acid?
options: An amino acid with two alpha-hydrogens
An amino acid with two substituents at the alpha-carbon, restricting conformational flexibility
An amino acid with two carboxyl groups
An amino acid with two amino groups
explanation: Alpha,alpha-disubstituted amino acids (e.g., Aib) restrict phi/psi angles, stabilizing specific secondary structures like helices and reducing conformational entropy.
